zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight於1230年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威可藍利卡爾德時，他的父親Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught37歲，母親Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught30歲。他於1255年與Agnes Ô’Brien在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威高威結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他於1263年登記服兵役。他去世於1270年7月28日在愛爾蘭庫迪 雷特利姆卡里克 奧 香農享年40歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 高威阿騰利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight于1230年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 高威可藍利卡爾德时，他的父亲Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught37岁，母亲Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught30岁。他于1255年与Agnes Ô’Brien在爱尔兰庫迪 高威高威结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他于1263年登记服兵役。他去世于1270年7月28日在爱尔兰庫迪 雷特利姆卡里克 奧 香農享年40岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 高威阿騰利。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Oge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Danish vernacular form of Aghi, a short form of the various names of Old Norse origin containing the element ag ‘edge (of a weapon)’ or ‘awe, terror’.

nameMeanings.name: "Mac
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Mabel .   See also  Maeve .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Mor"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name from mór ‘large, great’. This was the commonest of all girls’ names in late medieval Ireland, and has continued in use in both Ireland and Scotland to the present day.

nameMeanings.name: Atha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Arabic letters ṭa and ha, the opening letters of the twentieth sura in the Qur‘an.

nameMeanings.name: an
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Loving’.

nameMeanings.name: Chip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Wisdom’; ‘thousand blessings’.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight was born in 1230, in Clanricarde, County Galway, Ireland, his father, Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught, was 37 and his mother, Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, was 30. He married Agnes Ô’Brien in 1255, in Galway, County Galway, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He registered for military service in 1263. He died on 28 July 1270, in Carrick on Shannon, County Leitrim, Ireland, at the age of 40, and was buried in Athenry, County Galway, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight est né en 1230, à Clanricarde, County Galway, Irlande, son père, Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Ô’Brien en 1255, à Galway, County Galway, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1263. Il est décédé le 28 juillet 1270, à Carrick on Shannon, County Leitrim, Irlande, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Athenry, County Galway, Irlande.

Er hat 1255 in Galway, County Galway, Irland Agnes Ô’Brien geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er hat sich 1263 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 28. Juli 1270 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Carrick on Shannon, County Leitrim, Irland und wurde in Athenry, County Galway, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight è nato il nel 1230 nel Clanricarde, County Galway, Irlanda, il suo padre, Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Ô’Brien nel 1255, nel Galway, County Galway, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1263. ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knightが1230年にアイルランドコウントイ ガルワイクランリカードで生まれた時，父Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaughtは37歳，母Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaughtは30歳であった。1255年にアイルランドコウントイ ガルワイガルワイでAgnes Ô’Brienと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1263年に兵役登録をした。彼は1270年7月28日にアイルランドコウントイ レイトリムカーリック オン シャノンで40歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ガルワイアスエンリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight(이)가 1230년 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이 클랜리카드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1255년 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이 갈웨이에서 Agnes Ô’Brien(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1263년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1270년 7월 28일 아일랜드 카운티 레이트림 카릭  섀넌에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 갈웨이 앳헨리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight nasceu em 1230, em Clanricarde, County Galway, Irlanda, seu pai, Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Ô’Brien em 1255, em Galway, County Galway, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1263. Ele faleceu em 28 de julho de 1270, em Carrick on Shannon, County Leitrim, Irlanda, com 40 anos, e foi sepultado em Athenry, County Galway, Irlanda.

ru.Carrick on Shannon, County Leitrim, Irel: каррикк он шеннон, коунти литрим, Ирландия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Oge "Mac William Mor" Atha an Chip De Burgh knight родился в 1230 г. в местности кланрикард, коунти галвеи, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Richard Mór De Burgh 1st Baron of Connaught было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Egidia de Lacy Lady of Connaught, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Ô’Brien в 1255 г. в местности галвеи, коунти галвеи, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. В 1263 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 28 июля 1270 г. в местности каррикк он шеннон, коунти литрим, Ирландия в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности афенри, коунти галвеи, Ирландия.

Se casó con Agnes Ô’Brien en 1255, en Galway, County Galway, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1263. Murió el 28 de julio de 1270, en Carrick on Shannon, County Leitrim, Irlanda, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultado en Athenry, County Galway, Irlanda.
